untranslatable_reason: This signal requires contract expiration tracking, calendar spread mechanics (near-term short + far-term long on different contract months), and temporal logic (days until expiration, contract sequencing) that are not supported by the allowed indicators. The backtest engine requires time-series price data for individual contracts with expiration dates and the ability to manage multi-leg futures spreads across contract months. Standard price/technical indicators cannot express: (1) contract expiration dates, (2) relative positioning within contract lifecycle (X days before expiry), (3) spread month offsetting (12-month calendar spread), (4) rolling mechanics between successive contracts, or (5) simultaneous long/short positions on different contract expirations.
translation_notes: CHAN_AT_DRY_6 is a calendar spread strategy requiring futures contract infrastructure with expiration calendars and multi-contract position management. Parameters indicate: entry when near contract has 63-73 days to expiry (hold_days=63, days_before_expiry_entry=10 implies 63+10=73 days), exit after 63 days or 10 days before expiry (whichever first), with 12-month spread between legs. This requires architectural support beyond technical indicators.
